with momentum).\\n155: \\n156:     .. math::\\n157:        \\\\begin{aligned}\\n158:             &\\\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\\\\\\n159:             &\\\\textbf{input}      : \\\\gamma \\\\text{ (lr)}, \\\\: \\\\theta_0 \\\\text{ (params)}, \\\\: f(\\\\theta)\\n160:                 \\\\text{ (objective)}, \\\\: \\\\lambda \\\\text{ (weight decay)},                          \\\\\\\\\\n161:             &\\\\hspace{13mm} \\\\:\\\\mu \\\\text{ (momentum)}, \\\\:\\\\tau \\\\text{ (dampening)},\\n162:             \\\\:\\\\textit{ nesterov,}\\\\:\\\\textit{ maximize}                                     \\\\\\\\[-1.ex]\\n163:             &\\\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\\\\\\n164:             &\\\\textbf{for} \\\\: t=1 \\\\: \\\\textbf{to} \\\\: \\\\ldots \\\\: \\\\textbf{do}                         \\\\\\\\\\n165:             &\\\\hspace{5mm}\\\\textbf{if} \\\\: \\\\textit{maximize}:                                       \\\\\\\\\\n166:             &\\\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\\\leftarrow   -\\\\nabla_{\\\\theta} f_t (\\\\theta_{t-1})         \\\\\\\\\\n167:             &\\\\hspace{5mm}\\\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\\\\\\n168:             &\\\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\\\leftarrow   \\\\nabla_{\\\\theta} f_t (\\\\theta_{t-1})          \\\\\\\\\\n169:             &\\\\hspace{5mm}\\\\textbf{if} \\\\: \\\\lambda \\\\neq 0                                           \\\\\\\\\\n170:             &\\\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\\\leftarrow g_t + \\\\lambda  \\\\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\\\\\\n171:             &\\\\hspace{5mm}\\\\textbf{if} \\\\: \\\\mu \\\\neq 0                                               \\\\\\\\\\n172:             &\\\\hspace{10mm}\\\\textbf{if} \\\\: t > 1                                                   \\\\\\\\\\n173:             &\\\\hspace{15mm} \\\\textbf{b}_t \\\\leftarrow \\\\mu \\\\textbf{b}_{t-1} + (1-\\\\tau) g_t           \\\\\\\\\\n174:             &\\\\hspace{10mm}\\\\textbf{else}                                                          \\\\\\\\\\n175:             &\\\\hspace{15mm} \\\\textbf{b}_t \\\\leftarrow g_t                                           \\\\\\\\\\n176:             &\\\\hspace{10mm}\\\\textbf{if} \\\\: \\\\textit{nesterov}                                       \\\\\\\\\\n177:             &\\\\hspace{15mm} g_t \\\\leftarrow g_{t} + \\\\mu \\\\textbf{b}_t                               \\\\\\\\\\n178:             &\\\\hspace{10mm}\\\\textbf{else}                                                   \\\\\\\\[-1.ex]\\n179:             &\\\\hspace{15mm} g_t  \\\\leftarrow  \\\\textbf{b}_t                                         \\\\\\\\\\n180:             &\\\\hspace{5mm}\\\\theta_t \\\\leftarrow \\\\theta_{t-1} - \\\\gamma g_t                    \\\\\\\\[-1.ex]\\n181:             &\\\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\\\\\[-1.ex]\\n182:             &\\\\bf{return} \\\\:  \\\\theta_t                                                     \\\\\\\\[-1.ex]\\n183:             &\\\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\\\\\[-1.ex]\\n184:        \\\\end{aligned}\\n185: \\n186:     Nesterov momentum is based on the formula from\\n187:     `On the importance of initialization and momentum in deep learning`__.\\n188:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n189:     + rf\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n190:     Args:\\n191:         {_params_doc}\\n192:         lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-3)\\n193:         momentum (float, optional): momentum factor (default: 0)\\n194:         dampening (float, optional): dampening for momentum (default: 0)\\n195:         weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\\n196:         nesterov (bool, optional): enables Nesterov momentum. Only applicable\\n197:             when momentum is non-zero. (default: False)\\n198:         {_maximize_doc}\\n199:         {_foreach_doc}\\n200:         {_differentiable_doc}\\n201:         {_fused_doc}\\n202:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n203:     + r\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n204: \\n205:     Example:\\n206:         >>> # xdoctest: +SKIP\\n207:         >>> optimizer = torch.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1, momentum=0.9)\\n208:         >>> optimizer.zero_grad()\\n209:         >>> loss_fn(model(input), target).backward()\\n210:         >>> optimizer.step()\\n211: \\n212:     __ http://www.cs.toronto.edu/%7Ehinton/absps/momentum.pdf\\n213: \\n214:     .. note::\\n215:         The implementation of SGD with Momentum/Nesterov subtly differs from\\n216:         Sutskever et al. and implementations in some other frameworks.\\n217: \\n218:         Considering the specific case of Momentum, the update can be written as\\n219: \\n220:         .. math::\\n221:             \\\\begin{aligned}\\n222:                 v_{t+1} & = \\\\mu * v_{t} + g_{t+1}, \\\\\\\\\\n223:                 p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - \\\\text{lr} * v_{t+1},\\n224:             \\\\end{aligned}\\n225: \\n226:         where :math:`p`, :math:`g`, :math:`v` and :math:`\\\\mu` denote the\\n227:         parameters, gradient, velocity, and momentum respectively.\\n228: \\n229:         This is in contrast to Sutskever et al. and\\n230:         other frameworks which employ an update of the form\\n231: \\n232:         .. math::\\n233:             \\\\begin{aligned}\\n234:                 v_{t+1} & = \\\\mu * v_{t} + \\\\text{lr} * g_{t+1}, \\\\\\\\\\n235:                 p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - v_{t+1}.\\n236:             \\\\end{aligned}\\n237: \\n238:         The Nesterov version is analogously modified.\\n239: \\n240:         Moreover, the initial value of the momentum buffer is set to the\\n241:         gradient value at the first step. This is in contrast to some other\\n242:         frameworks that initialize it to all zeros. One notable side effect\\n243:         of this decision is that the first momentum value will not be scaled\\n244:         by dampening. Dampening will be applied starting at the second step.\\n245: \\n246:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n247: )\\n248: \\n249: \\n250: def sgd(\\n251:     params: list[Tensor],\\n252:     d_p_list: list[Tensor],\\n253:     momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],\\n254:     # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\\n255:     # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\\n256:     has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\\n257:     foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\\n258:     fused: Optional[bool] = None,\\n259:     grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,\\n260:     found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,\\n261:     *,\\n262:     weight_decay: float,\\n263:     momentum: float,\\n264:     lr: float,\\n265:     dampening: float,\\n266:     nesterov: bool,\\n267:     maximize: bool,\\n268: ):\\n269:     r\\\"\\\"\\\"Functional API that performs SGD algorithm computation.\\n270: \\n271:     See :class:`~torch.optim.SGD` for details.\\n272:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n273:     # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused. We only want to change\\n274:     # the default when neither have been user-specified. Note that we default to foreach\\n275:     # and pass False to use_fused. This is not a mistake--we want to give the fused impl\\n276:     # bake-in time before making it the default, even if it is typically faster.\\n277:     if foreach is None and fused is None:\\n278:         # why must we be explicit about an if statement for torch.jit.is_scripting here?\\n279:         # because JIT can't handle Optionals nor fancy conditionals when scripting\\n280:         if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\\n281:             fused, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\\n282:                 params, differentiable=False, use_fused=False\\n283:             )\\n284:         else:\\n285:             foreach = False\\n286:             fused = False\\n287:     if foreach is None:\\n288:         foreach = False\\n289:     if fused is None:\\n290:         fused = False\\n291: \\n292:     if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\\n293:         raise RuntimeError(\\\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\\\")\\n294:     if fused and torch.jit.is_scripting():\\n295:         raise RuntimeError(\\\"torch.jit.script not supported with fused optimizers\\\")\\n296: \\n297:     if foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\\n298:         func = _multi_tensor_sgd\\n299:     elif fused and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\\n300:         func = _fused_sgd\\n301:     else:\\n302:         func = _single_tensor_sgd\\n303: \\n304:     func(\\n305:         params,\\n306:         d_p_list,\\n307:         momentum_buffer_list,\\n308:         weight_decay=weight_decay,\\n309:         momentum=momentum,\\n310:         lr=lr,\\n311:         dampening=dampening,\\n312:         nesterov=nesterov,\\n313:         has_sparse_grad=has_sparse_grad,\\n314:         maximize=maximize,\\n315:         grad_scale=grad_scale,\\n316:         found_inf=found_inf,\\n317:     )\\n318: \\n319: \\n320: def _single_tensor_sgd(\\n321:     params: list[Tensor],\\n322:     grads: list[Tensor],\\n323:     momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],\\n324:     grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\\n325:     found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\\n326:     *,\\n327:     weight_decay: float,\\n328:     momentum: float,\\n329:     lr: float,\\n330:     dampening: float,\\n331:     nesterov: bool,\\n332:     maximize: bool,\\n333:     has_sparse_grad: bool,\\n334: ):\\n335:     assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\\n336: \\n337:     if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\\n338:         lr = _to_scalar(lr)\\n339: \\n340:     for i, param in enumerate(params):\\n341:         grad = grads[i] if not maximize else -grads[i]\\n342: \\n343:         if weight_decay != 0:\\n344:             # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\\n345:             if isinstance(weight_decay, Tensor):\\n346:                 if weight_decay.requires_grad:\\n347:                     # usually this is the differentiable path, which is why the param.clone() is needed\\n348:                     grad = grad.addcmul_(param.clone(), weight_decay)\\n349:                 else:\\n350:                     grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\\n351:             else:\\n352:                 grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\\n353: \\n354:         if momentum != 0:\\n355:             buf = momentum_buffer_list[i]\\n356: \\n357:             if buf is None:\\n358:                 buf = grad.detach().clone()\\n359:                 momentum_buffer_list[i] = buf\\n360:             else:\\n361:                 buf.mul_(momentum).add_(grad, alpha=1 - dampening)\\n362: \\n363:             if nesterov:\\n364:                 grad = grad.add(buf, alpha=momentum)\\n365:             else:\\n366:                 grad = buf\\n367: \\n368:         # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\\n369:         if isinstance(lr, Tensor):\\n370:             if lr.requires_grad:\\n371:                 param.addcmul_(grad, lr, value=-1)\\n372:             else:\\n373:                 param.add_(grad, alpha=-lr)\\n374:         else:\\n375:             param.add_(grad, alpha=-lr)\\n376: \\n377: \\n378: def _multi_tensor_sgd(\\n379:     params: list[Tensor],\\n380:     grads: float,\\n386:     momentum: float,\\n387:     lr: float,\\n388:     dampening: float,\\n389:     nesterov: bool,\\n390:     maximize: bool,\\n391:     has_sparse_grad: bool,\\n392: ):\\n393: with momentum).\\n155: \\n156:     .. math::\\n157:        \\\\begin{aligned}\\n158:             &\\\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\\\\\\n159:             &\\\\textbf{input}      : \\\\gamma \\\\text{ (lr)}, \\\\: \\\\theta_0 \\\\text{ (params)}, \\\\: f(\\\\theta)\\n160:                 \\\\text{ (objective)}, \\\\: \\\\lambda \\\\text{ (weight decay)},                          \\\\\\\\\\n161:             &\\\\hspace{13mm} \\\\:\\\\mu \\\\text{ (momentum)}, \\\\:\\\\tau \\\\text{ (dampening)},\\n162:             \\\\:\\\\textit{ nesterov,}\\\\:\\\\textit{ maximize}                                     \\\\\\\\[-1.ex]\\n163:             &\\\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\\\\\\n164:             &\\\\textbf{for} \\\\: t=1 \\\\: \\\\textbf{to} \\\\: \\\\ldots \\\\: \\\\textbf{do}                         \\\\\\\\\\n165:             &\\\\hspace{5mm}\\\\textbf{if} \\\\: \\\\textit{maximize}:                                       \\\\\\\\\\n166:             &\\\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\\\leftarrow   -\\\\nabla_{\\\\theta} f_t (\\\\theta_{t-1})         \\\\\\\\\\n167:             &\\\\hspace{5mm}\\\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\\\\\\n168:             &\\\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\\\leftarrow   \\\\nabla_{\\\\theta} f_t (\\\\theta_{t-1})          \\\\\\\\\\n169:             &\\\\hspace{5mm}\\\\textbf{if} \\\\: \\\\lambda \\\\neq 0                                           \\\\\\\\\\n170:             &\\\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\\\leftarrow g_t + \\\\lambda  \\\\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\\\\\\n171:             &\\\\hspace{5mm}\\\\textbf{if} \\\\: \\\\mu \\\\neq 0                                               \\\\\\\\\\n172:             &\\\\hspace{10mm}\\\\textbf{if} \\\\: t > 1                                                   \\\\\\\\\\n173:             &\\\\hspace{15mm} \\\\textbf{b}_t \\\\leftarrow \\\\mu \\\\textbf{b}_{t-1} + (1-\\\\tau) g_t           \\\\\\\\\\n174:             &\\\\hspace{10mm}\\\\textbf{else}                                                          \\\\\\\\\\n175:             &\\\\hspace{15mm} \\\\textbf{b}_t \\\\leftarrow g_t                                           \\\\\\\\\\n176:             &\\\\hspace{10mm}\\\\textbf{if} \\\\: \\\\textit{nesterov}                                       \\\\\\\\\\n177:             &\\\\hspace{15mm} g_t \\\\leftarrow g_{t} + \\\\mu \\\\textbf{b}_t                               \\\\\\\\\\n178:             &\\\\hspace{10mm}\\\\textbf{else}                                                   \\\\\\\\[-1.ex]\\n179:             &\\\\hspace{15mm} g_t  \\\\leftarrow  \\\\textbf{b}_t                                         \\\\\\\\\\n180:             &\\\\hspace{5mm}\\\\theta_t \\\\leftarrow \\\\theta_{t-1} - \\\\gamma g_t                    \\\\\\\\[-1.ex]\\n181:             &\\\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\\\\\[-1.ex]\\n182:             &\\\\bf{return} \\\\:  \\\\theta_t                                                     \\\\\\\\[-1.ex]\\n183:             &\\\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\\\\\[-1.ex]\\n184:        \\\\end{aligned}\\n185: \\n186:     Nesterov momentum is based on the formula from\\n187:     `On the importance of initialization and momentum in deep learning`__.\\n188:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n189:     + rf\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n190:     Args:\\n191:         {_params_doc}\\n192:         lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-3)\\n193:         momentum (float, optional): momentum factor (default: 0)\\n194:         dampening (float, optional): dampening for momentum (default: 0)\\n195:         weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\\n196:         nesterov (bool, optional): enables Nesterov momentum. Only applicable\\n197:             when momentum is non-zero. (default: False)\\n198:         {_maximize_doc}\\n199:         {_foreach_doc}\\n200:         {_differentiable_doc}\\n201:         {_fused_doc}\\n202:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n203:     + r\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n204: \\n205:     Example:\\n206:         >>> # xdoctest: +SKIP\\n207:         >>> optimizer = torch.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1, momentum=0.9)\\n208:         >>> optimizer.zero_grad()\\n209:         >>> loss_fn(model(input), target).backward()\\n210:         >>> optimizer.step()\\n211: \\n212:     __ http://www.cs.toronto.edu/%7Ehinton/absps/momentum.pdf\\n213: \\n214:     .. note::\\n215:         The implementation of SGD with Momentum/Nesterov subtly differs from\\n216:         Sutskever et al. and implementations in some other frameworks.\\n217: \\n218:         Considering the specific case of Momentum, the update can be written as\\n219: \\n220:         .. math::\\n221:             \\\\begin{aligned}\\n222:                 v_{t+1} & = \\\\mu * v_{t} + g_{t+1}, \\\\\\\\\\n223:                 p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - \\\\text{lr} * v_{t+1},\\n224:             \\\\end{aligned}\\n225: \\n226:         where :math:`p`, :math:`g`, :math:`v` and :math:`\\\\mu` denote the\\n227:         parameters, gradient, velocity, and momentum respectively.\\n228: \\n229:         This is in contrast to Sutskever et al. and\\n230:         other frameworks which employ an update of the form\\n231: \\n232:         .. math::\\n233:             \\\\begin{aligned}\\n234:                 v_{t+1} & = \\\\mu * v_{t} + \\\\text{lr} * g_{t+1}, \\\\\\\\\\n235:                 p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - v_{t+1}.\\n236:             \\\\end{aligned}\\n237: \\n238:         The Nesterov version is analogously modified.\\n239: \\n240:         Moreover, the initial value of the momentum buffer is set to the\\n241:         gradient value at the first step. This is in contrast to some other\\n242:         frameworks that initialize it to all zeros. One notable side effect\\n243:         of this decision is that the first momentum value will not be scaled\\n244:         by dampening. Dampening will be applied starting at the second step.\\n245: \\n246:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n247: )\\n248: \\n249: \\n250: def sgd(\\n251:     params: list[Tensor],\\n252:     d_p_list: list[Tensor],\\n253:     momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],\\n254:     # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\\n255:     # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\\n256:     has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\\n257:     foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\\n258:     fused: Optional[bool] = None,\\n259:     grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,\\n260:     found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,\\n261:     *,\\n262:     weight_decay: float,\\n263:     momentum: float,\\n264:     lr: float,\\n265:     dampening: float,\\n266:     nesterov: bool,\\n267:     maximize: bool,\\n268: ):\\n269:     r\\\"\\\"\\\"Functional API that performs SGD algorithm computation.\\n270: \\n271:     See :class:`~torch.optim.SGD` for details.\\n272:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n273:     # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused. We only want to change\\n274:     # the default when neither have been user-specified. Note that we default to foreach\\n275:     # and pass False to use_fused. This is not a mistake--we want to give the fused impl\\n276:     # bake-in time before making it the default, even if it is typically faster.\\n277:     if foreach is None and fused is None:\\n278:         # why must we be explicit about an if statement for torch.jit.is_scripting here?\\n279:         # because JIT can't handle Optionals nor fancy conditionals when scripting\\n280:         if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\\n281:             fused, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\\n282:                 params, differentiable=False, use_fused=False\\n283:             )\\n284:         else:\\n285:             foreach = False\\n286:             fused = False\\n287:     if foreach is None:\\n288:         foreach = False\\n289:     if fused is None:\\n290:         fused = False\\n291: \\n292:     if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\\n293:         raise RuntimeError(\\\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\\\")\\n294:     if fused and torch.jit.is_scripting():\\n295:         raise RuntimeError(\\\"torch.jit.script not supported with fused optimizers\\\")\\n296: \\n297:     if foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\\n298:         func = _multi_tensor_sgd\\n299:     elif fused and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\\n300:         func = _fused_sgd\\n301:     else:\\n302:         func = _single_tensor_sgd\\n303: \\n304: found_inf=found_inf,\\n317:     )\\n318: \\n319: \\n320: def _single_tensor_sgd(\\n321:     params: list[Tensor],\\n322:     grads: list[Tensor],\\n323:     momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],\\n324:     grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\\n325:     found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\\n326:     *,\\n327:     weight_decay: float,\\n328:     momentum: float,\\n329:     lr: float,\\n330:     dampening: float,\\n331:     nesterov: bool,\\n332:     maximize: bool,\\n333:     has_sparse_grad: bool,\\n334: ):\\n335:     assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\\n336: \\n337:     if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\\n338:         lr = _to_scalar(lr)\\n339: \\n340:     for i, param in enumerate(params):\\n341:         grad = grads[i] if not maximize else -grads[i]\\n342: \\n343:         if weight_decay != 0:\\n344:             # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\\n345:             if isinstance(weight_decay, Tensor):\\n346:                 if weight_decay.requires_grad:\\n347:                     # usually this is the differentiable path, which is why the param.clone() is needed\\n348:                     grad = grad.addcmul_(param.clone(), weight_decay)\\n349:                 else:\\n350:                     grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\\n351:             else:\\n352:                 grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\\n353: \\n354:         if momentum != 0:\\n355:             buf = momentum_buffer_list[i]\\n356: \\n357:             if buf is None:\\n358:                 buf = grad.detach().clone()\\n359:                 momentum_buffer_list[i] = buf\\n360:             else:\\n361:                 buf.mul_(momentum).add_(grad, alpha=1 - dampening)\\n362: \\n363: param.add_(grad, alpha=-lr)\\n374:         else:\\n375:             param.add_(grad, alpha=-lr)\\n376: \\n377: \\n378: def _multi_tensor_sgd(\\n379:     params: list[Tensor],\\n380:     grads:      device_params_,\\n406:         device_grads_,\\n407:         device_momentum_buffer_list,\\n408:     ), indices in grouped_tensors.values():\\n409:         device_params: list[Tensor] = math::\n157:        \\begin{aligned}\n158:             &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n159:             &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\: f(\\theta)\n160:                 \\text{ (objective)}, \\: \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},                          \\\\\n161:             &\\hspace{13mm} \\:\\mu \\text{ (momentum)}, \\:\\tau \\text{ (dampening)},\n162:             \\:\\textit{ nesterov,}\\:\\textit{ maximize}                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n163:             &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n164:             &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n165:             &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{maximize}:                                       \\\\\n166:             &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   -\\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})         \\\\\n167:             &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n168:             &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})          \\\\\n169:             &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                           \\\\\n170:             &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda  \\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\n171:             &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\mu \\neq 0                                               \\\\\n172:             &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{if} \\: t > 1                                                   \\\\\n173:             &\\hspace{15mm} \\textbf{b}_t \\leftarrow \\mu \\textbf{b}_{t-1} + (1-\\tau) g_t           \\\\\n174:             &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{else}                                                          \\\\\n175:             &\\hspace{15mm} \\textbf{b}_t \\leftarrow g_t                                           \\\\\n176:             &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{nesterov}                                       \\\\\n177:             &\\hspace{15mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_{t} + \\mu \\textbf{b}_t                               \\\\\n178:             &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{else}                                                   \\\\[-1.ex]\n179:             &\\hspace{15mm} g_t  \\leftarrow  \\textbf{b}_t                                         \\\\\n180:             &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma g_t                    \\\\[-1.ex]\n181:             &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n182:             &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n183:             &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n184:        \\end{aligned}\n185: \n186:     Nesterov momentum is based on the formula from\n187:     `On the importance of initialization and momentum in deep learning`__.\n188:     \"\"\"\n189:     + rf\"\"\"\n190:     Args:\n191:         {_params_doc}\n192:         lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-3)\n193:         momentum (float, optional): momentum factor (default: 0)\n194:         dampening (float, optional): dampening for momentum (default: 0)\n195:         weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n196:         nesterov (bool, optional): enables Nesterov momentum. Only applicable\n197:             when momentum is non-zero. (default: False)\n198:         {_maximize_doc}\n199:         {_foreach_doc}\n200:         {_differentiable_doc}\n201:         {_fused_doc}\n202:     \"\"\"\n203:     + r\"\"\"\n204: \n205:     Example:\n206:         >>> # xdoctest: +SKIP\n207:         >>> optimizer = torch.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1, momentum=0.9)\n208:         >>> optimizer.zero_grad()\n209:         >>> loss_fn(model(input), target).backward()\n210:         >>> optimizer.step()\n211: \n212:     __ http://www.cs.toronto.edu/%7Ehinton/absps/momentum.pdf\n213: \n214:     .. note::\n215:         The implementation of SGD with Momentum/Nesterov subtly differs from\n216:         Sutskever et al. and implementations in some other frameworks.\n217: \n218:         Considering the specific case of Momentum, the update can be written as\n219: \n220:         .. math::\n221:             \\begin{aligned}\n222:                 v_{t+1} & = \\mu * v_{t} + g_{t+1}, \\\\\n223:                 p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - \\text{lr} * v_{t+1},\n224:             \\end{aligned}\n225: \n226:         where :math:`p`, :math:`g`, :math:`v` and :math:`\\mu` denote the\n227:         parameters, gradient, velocity, and momentum respectively.\n228: \n229:         This is in contrast to Sutskever et al. and\n230:         other frameworks which employ an update of the form\n231: \n232:         .. math::\n233:             \\begin{aligned}\n234:                 v_{t+1} & = \\mu * v_{t} + \\text{lr} * g_{t+1}, \\\\\n235:                 p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - v_{t+1}.\n236:             \\end{aligned}\n237: \n238:         The Nesterov version is analogously modified.\n239: \n240:         Moreover, the initial value of the momentum buffer is set to the\n241:         gradient value at the first step. This is in contrast to some other\n242:         frameworks that initialize it to all zeros. One notable side effect\n243:         of this decision is that the first momentum value will not be scaled\n244:         by dampening. Dampening will be applied starting at the second step.\n245: \n246:     \"\"\"\n247: )\n248: \n249: \n250: def sgd(\n251:     params: list[Tensor],\n252:     d_p_list: list[Tensor],\n253:     momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],\n254:     # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n255:     # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n256:     has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\n257:     foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n258:     fused: Optional[bool] = None,\n259:     grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n260:     found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n261:     *,\n262:     weight_decay: float,\n263:     momentum: float,\n264:     lr: float,\n265:     dampening: float,\n266:     nesterov: bool,\n267:     maximize: bool,\n268: ):\n269:     r\"\"\"Functional API that performs SGD algorithm computation.\n270: \n271:     See :class:`~torch.optim.SGD` for details.\n272:     \"\"\"\n273:     # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused. and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\\n667:         # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\\n668:         if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and device_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\\n669:             torch._foreach_add_(\\n670:                 device_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\\\"cpu\\\"), alpha=1.0\\n671:             )\\n672:         else:\\n673:             torch._foreach_add_(device_state_steps, 1)\\n674: \\n675:                     device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\\n686:                     )\\n687: \\n688:         # Decay the first and second moment running average coefficient\\n689:         # device_beta1)\n693:         )\n694: \n695:         torch._foreach_mul_(device_exp_avg_sqs, beta2)\n696: \n697:         # Due to the strictness of the _foreach_addcmul API, we can't have a single\n698:         # tensor scalar as the scalar arg (only python number is supported there)\n699:         # as a result, separate out the value mul\n700:         # Filed https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/139795\n701:         if isinstance(beta2, torch.Tensor):\n702:             scaled_device_grads = torch._foreach_mul(device_grads, 1 - beta2)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n703:             value = 1.0\n704:         else:\n705:             scaled_device_grads = device_grads  # type: ignore[assignment]\n706:             value = 1 - beta2\n707: \n708:         torch._foreach_addcmul_(\n709:             device_exp_avg_sqs, scaled_device_grads, device_grads, value\n710:         )\n711: \n712:         # Delete the local intermediate(s) since they won't be used anymore to save on peak memory\n713:         del device_grads\n714:         del scaled_device_grads\n715: \n716:         bias_correction1: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n717:         bias_correction2: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n718:         bias_correction2_sqrt: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n719: \n720:         if capturable:\n721:             bias_correction1 = torch._foreach_pow(beta1, device_state_steps)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n722:             bias_correction2 = torch._foreach_pow(beta2, device_state_steps)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n723:             # foreach_sub doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg\n724:             torch._foreach_sub_(bias_correction1, 1)\n725:             torch._foreach_sub_(bias_correction2, 1)\n726:             # we do not negate bias_correction1 as it'll need to be negated later anyway\n727:             torch._foreach_neg_(bias_correction2)\n728: \n729:             # foreach_div doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg\n730:             torch._foreach_div_(bias_correction1, lr)\n731:             torch._foreach_reciprocal_(bias_correction1)\n732: \n733:             torch._foreach_sqrt_(bias_correction2)\n734: \n735:             # Re-assign for clarity as we maintain minimal intermediates: we'll have\n736:             # step_size = -         &\\\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\\\leftarrow g_t + \\\\lambda  \\\\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\\\\\\n171:             &\\\\hspace{5mm}\\\\textbf{if} \\\\: \\\\mu \\\\neq 0 \\\\textbf{b}_t \\\\leftarrow \\\\mu \\\\textbf{b}_{t-1} + (1-\\\\tau) g_t           \\\\\\\\\\n174:             &\\\\hspace{10mm}\\\\textbf{else}                                                          \\\\\\\\\\n175:             &\\\\hspace{15mm} \\\\textbf{b}_t \\\\leftarrow g_t                                           \\\\\\\\\\n176:             &\\\\hspace{10mm}\\\\textbf{if} \\\\: \\\\textit{nesterov}                                       \\\\\\\\\\n177:             &\\\\hspace{15mm} g_t \\\\leftarrow g_{t} + \\\\mu \\\\textbf{b}_t                               \\\\\\\\\\n178:             &\\\\hspace{10mm}\\\\textbf{else}                                                   \\\\\\\\[-1.ex]\\n179:             &\\\\hspace{15mm} g_t  \\\\leftarrow  \\\\textbf{b}_t                                         \\\\\\\\\\n180:             &\\\\hspace{5mm}\\\\theta_t \\\\leftarrow \\\\theta_{t-1} - \\\\gamma g_t                    \\\\\\\\[-1.ex]\\n181:             &\\\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\\\\\[-1.ex]\\n182:             &\\\\bf{return} \\\\:  \\\\theta_t                                                     \\\\\\\\[-1.ex]\\n183:             &\\\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\\\\\[-1.ex]\\n184:        \\\\end{aligned}\\n185: \\n186:     Nesterov momentum is based on the formula from\\n187:     `On the importance of initialization and momentum in deep learning`__.\\n188:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n189:     + rf\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n190:     Args:\\n191:         {_params_doc}\\n192:         lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-3)\\n193:         momentum (float, optional): momentum factor (default: 0)\\n194:         dampening (float, optional): dampening for momentum (default: 0)\\n195:         weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\\n196:         nesterov (bool, optional): enables Nesterov momentum. Only applicable\\n197:             when momentum is non-zero. (default: False)\\n198:         {_maximize_doc}\\n199:         {_foreach_doc}\\n200:         {_differentiable_doc}\\n201:         {_fused_doc}\\n202:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n203:     + r\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n204: \\n205:     Example:\\n206:         >>> # xdoctest: +SKIP\\n207:         >>> optimizer = torch.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1, momentum=0.9)\\n208:         >>> optimizer.zero_grad()\\n209:         >>> loss_fn(model(input), target).backward()\\n210:         >>> optimizer.step()\\n211: \\n212:     __ http://www.cs.toronto.edu/%7Ehinton/absps/momentum.pdf\\n213: \\n214:     .. note::\\n215:         The implementation of SGD with Momentum/Nesterov subtly differs from\\n216:         Sutskever et al. and implementations in some other frameworks.\\n217: \\n218:         Considering the specific case of Momentum, the update can be written as\\n219: \\n220:         .. math::\\n221:             \\\\begin{aligned}\\n222:                 v_{t+1} & = \\\\mu * v_{t} + g_{t+1}, \\\\\\\\\\n223:                 p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - \\\\text{lr} * v_{t+1},\\n224:             \\\\end{aligned}\\n225: \\n226:         where :math:`p`, :math:`g`, :math:`v` and :math:`\\\\mu` denote the\\n227:         parameters, gradient, velocity, and momentum respectively.\\n228: \\n229:         This is in contrast to Sutskever et al. and\\n230:         other frameworks which employ an update of the form\\n231: \\n232:         .. math::\\n233:             \\\\begin{aligned}\\n234:                 v_{t+1} & = \\\\mu * v_{t} + \\\\text{lr} * g_{t+1}, \\\\\\\\\\n235:                 p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - v_{t+1}.\\n236:             \\\\end{aligned}\\n237: \\n238:         The Nesterov version is analogously modified.\\n239: \\n240:         Moreover, the initial value of the momentum buffer is set to the\\n241:         gradient value at the first step. This is in contrast to some other\\n242:         frameworks that initialize it to all zeros. One notable side effect\\n243:         of this decision is that the first momentum value will not be scaled\\n244:         by dampening. Dampening will be applied starting at the second step.\\n245: \\n246:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n247: )\\n248: \\n249: \\n250: def sgd(\\n251:     params: list[Tensor],\\n252:     d_p_list: list[Tensor],\\n253:     momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],\\n254:     # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\\n255:     # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\\n256:     has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\\n257:     foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\\n258:     fused: Optional[bool] = None,\\n259:     grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,\\n260:     found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,\\n261:     *,\\n262:     weight_decay: float,\\n263:     momentum: float,\\n264:     lr: float,\\n265:     dampening: float,\\n266:     nesterov: bool,\\n267:     maximize: bool,\\n268: ):\\n269:     r\\\"\\\"\\\"Functional API that performs SGD algorithm computation.\\n270: \\n271:     See :class:`~torch.optim.SGD` for details.\\n272:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n273:     # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused. We only want to change\\n274:     # the default when neither have been user-specified. Note that we default to foreach\\n275:     # and pass False to use_fused. This is not a mistake--we want to give the fused impl\\n276:     # bake-in time before making it the default, even if it is typically faster.\\n277:     if foreach is None and fused is None:\\n278:         # why must we be explicit about an if statement for torch.jit.is_scripting here?\\n279:         # because JIT can't handle Optionals nor fancy conditionals when scripting\\n280:         if not else:\\n285:             foreach = False\\n286:             fused = False\\n287:     if foreach is None:\\n288:         foreach = False\\n289:     if fused is None:\\n290:         fused = False\\n291: \\n292:     if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\\n293:         raise RuntimeError(\\\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\\\")\\n294:     if fused and func = _multi_tensor_sgd\\n299:     elif fused and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\\n300:         func = _fused_sgd\\n301:     else:\\n302:         func = _single_tensor_sgd\\n303: \\n304: with momentum).\\n155: \\n156:     .. math::\\n157:        \\\\begin{aligned}\\n158:             &\\\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\\\\\\n159:             &\\\\textbf{input}      : \\\\gamma \\\\text{ (lr)}, \\\\: \\\\theta_0 \\\\text{ (params)}, \\\\: f(\\\\theta)\\n160:                 \\\\text{ (objective)}, \\\\: \\\\lambda \\\\text{         &\\\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\\\leftarrow g_t + \\\\lambda  \\\\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\\\\\\n171:             &\\\\hspace{5mm}\\\\textbf{if} \\\\: \\\\mu \\\\neq 0    \\\\\\\\\\n175:             &\\\\hspace{15mm} \\\\textbf{b}_t \\\\leftarrow g_t                                           \\\\\\\\\\n176:             &\\\\hspace{10mm}\\\\textbf{if} \\\\: \\\\textit{nesterov}                                       \\\\\\\\\\n177:             &\\\\hspace{15mm} g_t \\\\leftarrow g_{t} + \\\\mu \\\\textbf{b}_t                               \\\\\\\\\\n178:             &\\\\hspace{10mm}\\\\textbf{else}                                                   \\\\\\\\[-1.ex]\\n179:             &\\\\hspace{15mm} g_t  \\\\leftarrow  \\\\textbf{b}_t                                         \\\\\\\\\\n180:             &\\\\hspace{5mm}\\\\theta_t \\\\leftarrow \\\\theta_{t-1} - \\\\gamma g_t                    \\\\\\\\[-1.ex]\\n181:             &\\\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\\\\\[-1.ex]\\n182:             &\\\\bf{return} \\\\:  \\\\theta_t                                                     \\\\\\\\[-1.ex]\\n183:             &\\\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\\\\\[-1.ex]\\n184:        \\\\end{aligned}\\n185: \\n186:     Nesterov momentum is based on the formula from\\n187:     `On the importance of initialization and momentum in deep learning`__.\\n188:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n189:     + rf\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n190:     Args:\\n191:         {_params_doc}\\n192:         lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-3)\\n193:         momentum (float, optional): momentum factor (default: 0)\\n194:         dampening (float, optional): dampening for momentum (default: 0)\\n195:         weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\\n196:         nesterov (bool, optional): enables Nesterov momentum. Only applicable\\n197:             when momentum is non-zero. (default: False)\\n198:         {_maximize_doc}\\n199:         {_foreach_doc}\\n200:         {_differentiable_doc}\\n201:         {_fused_doc}\\n202:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n203:     + r\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n204: \\n205:     Example:\\n206:         >>> # xdoctest: +SKIP\\n207:         >>> optimizer = torch.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1, momentum=0.9)\\n208:         >>> optimizer.zero_grad()\\n209:         >>> loss_fn(model(input), target).backward()\\n210:         >>> optimizer.step()\\n211: \\n212:     __ http://www.cs.toronto.edu/%7Ehinton/absps/momentum.pdf\\n213: \\n214:     .. note::\\n215:         The implementation of SGD with Momentum/Nesterov subtly differs from\\n216:         Sutskever et al. and implementations in some other frameworks.\\n217: \\n218:         Considering the specific case of Momentum, the update can be written as\\n219: \\n220:         .. math::\\n221:             \\\\begin{aligned}\\n222:                 v_{t+1} & = \\\\mu * v_{t} + g_{t+1}, \\\\\\\\\\n223:                 p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - \\\\text{lr} * v_{t+1},\\n224:             \\\\end{aligned}\\n225: \\n226:         where :math:`p`, :math:`g`, :math:`v` and :math:`\\\\mu` denote the\\n227:         parameters, gradient, velocity, and momentum respectively.\\n228: \\n229:         This is in contrast to Sutskever et al. and\\n230:         other frameworks which employ an update of the form\\n231: \\n232:         .. math::\\n233:             \\\\begin{aligned}\\n234:                 v_{t+1} & = \\\\mu * v_{t} + \\\\text{lr} * g_{t+1}, \\\\\\\\\\n235:                 p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - v_{t+1}.\\n236:             \\\\end{aligned}\\n237: \\n238:         The Nesterov version is analogously modified.\\n239: \\n240:         Moreover, the initial value of the momentum buffer is set to the\\n241:         gradient value at the first step. This is in contrast to some other\\n242:         frameworks that initialize it to all zeros. One notable side effect\\n243:         of this decision is that the first momentum value will not be scaled\\n244:         by dampening. Dampening will be applied starting at the second step.\\n245: \\n246:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n247: )\\n248: \\n249: \\n250: def sgd(\\n251:     params: list[Tensor],\\n252:     d_p_list: list[Tensor],\\n253:     momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],\\n254:     # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\\n255:     # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\\n256:     has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\\n257:     foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\\n258:     fused: Optional[bool] = None,\\n259:     grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,\\n260:     found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,\\n261:     *,\\n262:     weight_decay: float,\\n263:     momentum: float,\\n264:     lr: float,\\n265:     dampening: float,\\n266:     nesterov: bool,\\n267:     maximize: bool,\\n268: ):\\n269:     r\\\"\\\"\\\"Functional API that performs SGD algorithm computation.\\n270: \\n271:     See :class:`~torch.optim.SGD` for details.\\n272:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n273:     # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused. We only want to change\\n274:     # the default when neither have been user-specified. Note that we default to foreach\\n275:     # and pass False to use_fused. This is not a mistake--we want to give the fused impl\\n276:     # bake-in time before making it the default, even if it is typically faster.\\n277:     if foreach is None and fused is None:\\n278: math::\n157:        \\begin{aligned}\n158:             &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n159:             &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\: f(\\theta)\n160:                 \\text{ (objective)}, \\: \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},                          \\\\\n161:             &\\hspace{13mm} \\:\\mu \\text{ (momentum)}, \\:\\tau \\text{ (dampening)},\n162:             \\:\\textit{ nesterov,}\\:\\textit{ maximize}                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n163:             &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n164:             &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n165:             &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{maximize}:                                       \\\\\n166:             &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   -\\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})         \\\\\n167:             &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n168:             &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})          \\\\\n169:             &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                           \\\\\n170:             &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda  \\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\n171:             &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\mu \\neq 0                                               \\\\\n172:             &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{if} \\: t > 1                                                   \\\\\n173:             &\\hspace{15mm} \\textbf{b}_t \\leftarrow \\mu \\textbf{b}_{t-1} + (1-\\tau) g_t           \\\\\n174:             &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{else}                                                          \\\\\n175:             &\\hspace{15mm} \\textbf{b}_t \\leftarrow g_t                                           \\\\\n176:             &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{nesterov}                                       \\\\\n177:             &\\hspace{15mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_{t} + \\mu \\textbf{b}_t                               \\\\\n178:             &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{else}                                                   \\\\[-1.ex]\n179:             &\\hspace{15mm} g_t  \\leftarrow  \\textbf{b}_t                                         \\\\\n180:             &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma g_t                    \\\\[-1.ex]\n181:             &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n182:             &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n183:             &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n184:        \\end{aligned}\n185: \n186:     Nesterov momentum is based on the formula from\n187:     `On the importance of initialization and momentum in deep learning`__.\n188:     \"\"\"\n189:     + rf\"\"\"\n190:     Args:\n191:         {_params_doc}\n192:         lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-3)\n193:         momentum (float, optional): momentum factor (default: 0)\n194:         dampening (float, optional): dampening for momentum (default: 0)\n195:         weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n196:         nesterov (bool, optional): enables Nesterov momentum. Only applicable\n197:             when momentum is non-zero. (default: False)\n198:         {_maximize_doc}\n199:         {_foreach_doc}\n200:         {_differentiable_doc}\n201:         {_fused_doc}\n202:     \"\"\"\n203:     + r\"\"\"\n204: \n205:     Example:\n206:         >>> # xdoctest: +SKIP\n207:         >>> optimizer = torch.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1, momentum=0.9)\n208:         >>> optimizer.zero_grad()\n209:         >>> loss_fn(model(input), target).backward()\n210:         >>> optimizer.step()\n211: \n212:     __ http://www.cs.toronto.edu/%7Ehinton/absps/momentum.pdf\n213: \n214:     .. note::\n215:         The implementation of SGD with Momentum/Nesterov subtly differs from\n216:         Sutskever et al. and implementations in some other frameworks.\n217: \n218:         Considering the specific case of Momentum, the update can be written as\n219: \n220:         .. math::\n221:             \\begin{aligned}\n222:                 v_{t+1} & = \\mu * v_{t} + g_{t+1}, \\\\\n223:                 p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - \\text{lr} * v_{t+1},\n224:             \\end{aligned}\n225: \n226:         where :math:`p`, :math:`g`, :math:`v` and :math:`\\mu` denote the\n227:         parameters, gradient, velocity, and momentum respectively.\n228: \n229:         This is in contrast to Sutskever et al. and\n230:         other frameworks which employ an update of the form\n231: \n232:         .. math::\n233:             \\begin{aligned}\n234:                 v_{t+1} & = \\mu * v_{t} + \\text{lr} * g_{t+1}, \\\\\n235:                 p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - v_{t+1}.\n236:             \\end{aligned}\n237: \n238:         The Nesterov version is analogously modified.\n239: \n240:         Moreover, the initial value of the momentum buffer is set to the\n241:         gradient value at the first step. This is in contrast to some other\n242:         frameworks that initialize it to all zeros. One notable side effect\n243:         of this decision is that the first momentum value will not be scaled\n244:         by dampening. Dampening will be applied starting at the second step.\n245: \n246:     \"\"\"\n247: )\n248: \n249: \n250: def sgd(\n251:     params: list[Tensor],\n252:     d_p_list: list[Tensor],\n253:     momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],\n254:     # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n255:     # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n256:     has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\n257:     foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n258:     fused: Optional[bool] = None,\n259:     grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n260:     found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n261:     *,\n262:     weight_decay: float,\n263:     momentum: float,\n264:     lr: float,\n265:     dampening: float,\n266:     nesterov: bool,\n267:     maximize: bool,\n268: ):\n269:     r\"\"\"Functional API that performs SGD algorithm computation.\n270: \n271:     See :class:`~torch.optim.SGD` for details.\n272:     \"\"\"\n273:     # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused. mypy: allow-untyped-defs\nr\"\"\"Implementation for Stochastic Gradient Descent optimizer.\"\"\"\n\nfrom typing import cast, Optional, Union\n\nimport torch\nfrom torch import Tensor\n\nfrom super().__init__(params, defaults)\n\n        if fused:\n            self._step_supports_amp_scaling = True\n            self._need_device_dtype_check_for_fused = True\n            if differentiable:\n                raise RuntimeError(\"`fused` does not support `differentiable`\")\n            if foreach:\n                raise RuntimeError(\"`fused` and `foreach` cannot be `True` together.\")\n\n    def __setstate__(self, state):  # noqa: D105\n        super().__setstate__(state)\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            group.setdefault(\"nesterov\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"maximize\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"foreach\", None)\n            group.setdefault(\"differentiable\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"fused\", False)\n\n    def _init_group(self, group, params, grads, momentum_buffer_list):\n        has_sparse_grad = False\n\n        for p in group[\"params\"]:\n            if p.grad is not None:\n                if group[\"fused\"] and getattr(\n                    self, \"_need_device_dtype_check_for_fused\", True\n                ):\n                    _device_dtype_check_for_fused(p)\n                    self._need_device_dtype_check_for_fused = False\n                params.append(p)\n                grads.append(p.grad)\n                if p.grad.is_sparse:\n                    has_sparse_grad = True\n\n                if group[\"momentum\"] != 0:\n                    state = self.state[p]\n                    momentum_buffer_list.append(state.get(\"momentum_buffer\"))\n\n        return has_sparse_grad\n\n    @_use_grad_for_differentiable\n    def step(self, closure=None):\n        \"\"\"Perform a single optimization step.\n\n        Args:\n            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n                and returns the loss.\n        \"\"\"\n        loss = None\n        if closure is not None:\n            with torch.enable_grad():\n                loss = closure()\n\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            params: list[Tensor] = []\n            grads: list[Tensor] = []\n            momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]] = []\n\n            has_sparse_grad = self._init_group(\n                group, params, grads, momentum_buffer_list\n            )\n\n            sgd(\n                params,\n                grads,\n                momentum_buffer_list,\n                weight_decay=group[\"weight_decay\"],\n                momentum=group[\"momentum\"],\n                lr=group[\"lr\"],\n                dampening=group[\"dampening\"],\n                nesterov=group[\"nesterov\"],\n                maximize=group[\"maximize\"],\n                has_sparse_grad=has_sparse_grad,\n                foreach=group[\"foreach\"],\n                fused=group[\"fused\"],\n                grad_scale=getattr(self, \"grad_scale\", None),\n                found_inf=getattr(self, \"found_inf\", None),\n            )\n\n            if group[\"momentum\"] != 0:\n                # update momentum_buffers in state\n                for p, momentum_buffer in zip(params, momentum_buffer_list):\n                    state = self.state[p]\n                    state[\"momentum_buffer\"] = momentum_buffer\n\n        return loss\n\n\nSGD.__doc__ = (\n    r\"\"\"Implements stochastic gradient descent (optionally with momentum).\n\n    .. math::\n       \\begin{aligned}\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\: f(\\theta)\n                \\text{ (objective)}, \\: \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},                          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{13mm} \\:\\mu \\text{ (momentum)}, \\:\\tau \\text{ (dampening)},\n            \\:\\textit{ nesterov,}\\:\\textit{ maximize}                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{maximize}:                                       \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   -\\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda  \\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\mu \\neq 0                                               \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{if} \\: t > 1                                                   \\\\\n            &\\hspace{15mm} \\textbf{b}_t \\leftarrow \\mu \\textbf{b}_{t-1} + (1-\\tau) g_t           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{else}                                                          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{15mm} \\textbf{b}_t \\leftarrow g_t                                           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{nesterov}                                       \\\\\n            &\\hspace{15mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_{t} + \\mu \\textbf{b}_t                               \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{else}                                                   \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\hspace{15mm} g_t  \\leftarrow  \\textbf{b}_t                                         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma g_t                    \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n       \\end{aligned}\n\n    Nesterov momentum is based on the formula from\n    `On the importance of initialization and momentum in deep learning`__.\n    \"\"\"\n    + rf\"\"\"\n    Args:\n        {_params_doc}\n        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-3)\n        momentum (float, optional): momentum factor (default: 0)\n        dampening (float, optional): dampening for momentum (default: 0)\n        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n        nesterov (bool, optional): enables Nesterov momentum. Only applicable\n            when momentum is non-zero. (default: False)\n        {_maximize_doc}\n        {_foreach_doc}\n        {_differentiable_doc}\n        {_fused_doc}\n    \"\"\"\n    + r\"\"\"\n\n    Example:\n        >>> # xdoctest: +SKIP\n        >>> optimizer = torch.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1, momentum=0.9)\n        >>> optimizer.zero_grad()\n        >>> loss_fn(model(input), target).backward()\n        >>> optimizer.step()\n\n    __ http://www.cs.toronto.edu/%7Ehinton/absps/momentum.pdf\n\n    .. note::\n        The implementation of SGD with Momentum/Nesterov subtly differs from\n        Sutskever et al. and implementations in some other frameworks.\n\n        Considering the specific case of Momentum, the update can be written as\n\n        .. math::\n            \\begin{aligned}\n                v_{t+1} & = \\mu * v_{t} + g_{t+1}, \\\\\n                p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - \\text{lr} * v_{t+1},\n            \\end{aligned}\n\n        where :math:`p`, :math:`g`, :math:`v` and :math:`\\mu` denote the\n        parameters, gradient, velocity, and momentum respectively.\n\n        This is in contrast to Sutskever et al. and\n        other frameworks which employ an update of the form\n\n        .. math::\n            \\begin{aligned}\n                v_{t+1} & = \\mu * v_{t} + \\text{lr} * g_{t+1}, \\\\\n                p_{t+1} & = p_{t} - v_{t+1}.\n            \\end{aligned}\n\n        The Nesterov version is analogously modified.\n\n        Moreover, the initial value of the momentum buffer is set to the\n        gradient value at the first step. This is in contrast to some other\n        frameworks that initialize it to all zeros. One notable side effect\n        of this decision is that the first momentum value will not be scaled\n        by dampening. Dampening will be applied starting at the second step.\n\n    \"\"\"\n)\n\n\ndef sgd(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    d_p_list: list[Tensor],\n    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],\n    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n    has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\n    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    fused: Optional[bool] = None,\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n    *,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    momentum: float,\n    lr: float,\n    dampening: float,\n    nesterov: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n):\n    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs SGD algorithm computation.\n\n    See :class:`~torch.optim.SGD` for details.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused. We only want to change\n    # the default when neither have been user-specified. Note that we default to foreach\n    # and pass False to use_fused. This is not a mistake--we want to give the fused impl\n    # bake-in time before making it the default, even if it is typically faster.\n    if foreach is None and fused is None:\n        # why must we be explicit about an if statement for torch.jit.is_scripting here?\n        # because JIT can't handle Optionals nor fancy conditionals when scripting\n        if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n            fused, foreach = _default_to_fused_or_foreach(\n                params, differentiable=False, use_fused=False\n            )\n        else:\n            foreach = False\n            fused = False\n    if foreach is None:\n        foreach = False\n    if fused is None:\n        fused = False\n\n    if foreach and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n    if fused and torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        raise RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with fused optimizers\")\n\n    if foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _multi_tensor_sgd\n    elif fused and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        func = _fused_sgd\n    else:\n        func = _single_tensor_sgd\n\n    func(\n        params,\n        d_p_list,\n        momentum_buffer_list,\n        weight_decay=weight_decay,\n        momentum=momentum,\n        lr=lr,\n        dampening=dampening,\n        nesterov=nesterov,\n        has_sparse_grad=has_sparse_grad,\n        maximize=maximize,\n        grad_scale=grad_scale,\n        found_inf=found_inf,\n    )\n\n\ndef _single_tensor_sgd(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    momentum: float,\n    lr: float,\n    dampening: float,\n    nesterov: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n):\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n        grad = grads[i] if not maximize else -grads[i]\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n            if isinstance(weight_decay, Tensor):\n                if weight_decay.requires_grad:\n                    # usually this is the differentiable path, which is why the param.clone() is needed\n                    grad = grad.addcmul_(param.clone(), weight_decay)\n                else:\n                    grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n            else:\n                grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n\n        if momentum != 0:\n            buf = momentum_buffer_list[i]\n\n            if buf is None:\n                buf = grad.detach().clone()\n                momentum_buffer_list[i] = buf\n            else:\n                buf.mul_(momentum).add_(grad, alpha=1 - dampening)\n\n            if nesterov:\n                grad = grad.add(buf, alpha=momentum)\n            else:\n                grad = buf\n\n        # Nested if is necessary to bypass jitscript rules\n        if isinstance(lr, Tensor):\n            if lr.requires_grad:\n                param.addcmul_(grad, lr, value=-1)\n            else:\n                param.add_(grad, alpha=-lr)\n        else:\n            param.add_(grad, alpha=-lr)\n\n\ndef _multi_tensor_sgd(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    momentum: float,\n    lr: float,\n    dampening: float,\n    nesterov: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n):\n    assert grad_scale is None and found_inf is None\n\n    if len(params) == 0:\n        return\n\n    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, momentum_buffer_list],  # type: ignore[list-item]\n        with_indices=True,\n    )\n    for (\n        device_params_,\n        device_grads_,\n        device_momentum_buffer_list,\n    ), indices in grouped_tensors.values():\n        device_params: list[Tensor] = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads: list[Tensor] = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n\n        device_has_sparse_grad = has_sparse_grad and any(\n            grad.is_sparse for grad in device_grads\n        )\n\n        if maximize:\n            device_grads = torch._foreach_neg(device_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            # Reuse the intermediate memory (device_grads) already allocated for maximize\n            if maximize:\n                torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n            else:\n                device_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                    device_grads, device_params, alpha=weight_decay\n                )\n\n        if momentum != 0:\n            bufs: list[Tensor] = []\n\n            all_states_with_momentum_buffer = True\n            for i in range(len(device_momentum_buffer_list)):\n                if device_momentum_buffer_list[i] is None:\n                    all_states_with_momentum_buffer = False\n                    break\n                else:\n                    bufs.append(cast(Tensor, device_momentum_buffer_list[i]))\n\n            if all_states_with_momentum_buffer:\n                torch._foreach_mul_(bufs, momentum)\n                torch._foreach_add_(bufs, device_grads, alpha=1 - dampening)\n            else:\n                bufs = []\n                for i in range(len(device_momentum_buffer_list)):\n                    if device_momentum_buffer_list[i] is None:\n                        buf = device_momentum_buffer_list[i] = momentum_buffer_list[\n                            indices[i]\n                        ] = device_grads[i].detach().clone()\n                    else:\n                        buf = cast(Tensor, device_momentum_buffer_list[i])\n                        buf.mul_(momentum).add_(device_grads[i], alpha=1 - dampening)\n\n                    bufs.append(buf)\n\n            if nesterov:\n                torch._foreach_add_(device_grads, bufs, alpha=momentum)\n            else:\n                device_grads = bufs\n\n        if not device_has_sparse_grad:\n            # handle internal item() call if lr is a tensor\n            if isinstance(lr, torch.Tensor) and torch.compiler.is_compiling():\n                grads_x_lr = torch._foreach_mul(device_grads, -lr)\n                torch._foreach_add_(device_params, grads_x_lr)\n            else:\n                torch._foreach_add_(device_params, device_grads, alpha=-lr)\n        else:\n            # foreach APIs don't support sparse\n            for i in range(len(device_params)):\n                device_params[i].add_(device_grads[i], alpha=-lr)\n\n\ndef _fused_sgd(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    grads: list[Tensor],\n    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n    *,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    momentum: float,\n    lr: float,\n    dampening: float,\n    nesterov: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n    has_sparse_grad: bool,\n) -> None:\n    if not params:\n        return\n    if has_sparse_grad:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"`_fused_sgd` does not support sparse gradients\")\n    grad_scale_dict: DeviceDict = (\n        {grad_scale.device: grad_scale} if grad_scale is not None else {}\n    )\n    found_inf_dict: DeviceDict = (\n        {found_inf.device: found_inf} if found_inf is not None else {}\n    )\n\n    no_momentum_buffer = momentum == 0\n    is_first_step = (\n        all(t is None for t in momentum_buffer_list) and not no_momentum_buffer\n    )\n    if is_first_step:\n        for i, g in enumerate(grads):\n            momentum_buffer_list[i] = torch.empty_like(g)\n    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        [params, grads, momentum_buffer_list],  # type: ignore[list-item]\n        with_indices=False,\n    )\n    for (device, _), (\n        (device_params_, device_grads_, device_momentum_buffer_list),\n        _,\n    ) in grouped_tensors.items():\n        device_params: list[Tensor] = cast(list[Tensor], device_params_)\n        device_grads: list[Tensor] = cast(list[Tensor], device_grads_)\n        device_grad_scale, device_found_inf = None, None\n        if grad_scale is not None:\n            device_grad_scale = grad_scale_dict.setdefault(\n                device, grad_scale.to(device)\n            )\n        if found_inf_dict is not None and found_inf is not None:\n            device_found_inf = found_inf_dict.setdefault(device, found_inf.to(device))\n        torch._fused_sgd_(\n            device_params,\n            device_grads,\n            []\n            if no_momentum_buffer\n            else cast(list[Tensor], device_momentum_buffer_list),\n            weight_decay=weight_decay,\n            momentum=momentum,\n            lr=lr,\n            dampening=dampening,\n            nesterov=nesterov,\n            maximize=maximize,\n            is_first_step=is_first_step,\n            grad_scale=device_grad_scale,\n            found_inf=device_found_inf,\n        )\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":5,"cache_creation_input_tokens":8270,"cache_read_input_tokens":63927,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":8270},"output_tokens":3,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"953fa671-031d-46c4-954c-c7cfd07696dd","uuid":"5b7e39f5-18b1-43b9-9aff-1688494c5d2e"}

weight_decay,\n            \"nesterov\": nesterov,\n            \"maximize\": maximize,\n            \"foreach\": foreach,\n            \"differentiable\": differentiable,\n            \"fused\": fused,\n        }\n        if nesterov and (momentum <= 0 or dampening != 0):\n            raise ValueError(\"Nesterov momentum requires a momentum and zero dampening\")\n differentiable:\n                raise RuntimeError(\"`fused` does not support `differentiable`\")\n            if foreach:\n                raise RuntimeError(\"`fused` and `foreach` cannot be `True` together.\")\n\n    def __setstate__(self, state):  # noqa: D105\n        super().__setstate__(state)\n        for group in self.param_groups:\n            group.setdefault(\"nesterov\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"maximize\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"foreach\", None)\n            group.setdefault(\"differentiable\", False)\n            group.setdefault(\"fused\", False)\n\n    def _init_group(self, group, params, grads, momentum_buffer_list):\n        has_sparse_grad = False\n\n        for p in group[\"params\"]:\n            if p.grad is not None:\n                if group[\"fused\"] and getattr(\n                    self, \"_need_device_dtype_check_for_fused\", True\n                ):\n                    _device_dtype_check_for_fused(p)\n                    self._need_device_dtype_check_for_fused = False\n                params.append(p)\n                grads.append(p.grad)\n                if p.grad.is_sparse:\n                    has_sparse_grad = closure that reevaluates the model\n                and returns the loss.\n        \"\"\"\n        loss = None\n        if closure is not None:\n            with torch.enable_grad():\n with momentum).\n\n    .. math::\n       \\begin{aligned}\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\: f(\\theta)\n                \\text{ (objective)}, \\: \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},                          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{13mm} \\:\\mu \\text{ (momentum)}, \\:\\tau \\text{ (dampening)},\n            \\:\\textit{ nesterov,}\\:\\textit{ maximize}                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{maximize}:                                       \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   -\\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda  \\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\mu \\neq 0                                               \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{if} \\: t > 1                                                   \\\\\n            &\\hspace{15mm} \\textbf{b}_t \\leftarrow \\mu \\textbf{b}_{t-1} + (1-\\tau) g_t           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{else}                                                          \\\\\n            &\\hspace{15mm} \\textbf{b}_t \\leftarrow g_t                                           \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{nesterov}                                       \\\\\n            &\\hspace{15mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_{t} + \\mu \\textbf{b}_t                               \\\\\n            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{else}                                                   \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\hspace{15mm} g_t  \\leftarrow  \\textbf{b}_t                                         \\\\\n            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma g_t                    \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n       \\end{aligned}\n\n    Nesterov momentum is based on the formula from\n    `On the importance of initialization and momentum in deep learning`__.\n    \"\"\"\n    + rf\"\"\"\n    Args:\n        {_params_doc}\n        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-3)\n        momentum (float, optional): momentum factor Dampening will be applied starting at the second step.\n\n    \"\"\"\n)\n\n\ndef sgd(\n    params: list[Tensor],\n    d_p_list: list[Tensor],\n    momentum_buffer_list: list[Optional[Tensor]],\n    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n    has_sparse_grad: bool = False,\n    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n    fused: Optional[bool] = None,\n    grad_scale: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n    found_inf: Optional[Tensor] = None,\n    *,\n    weight_decay: float,\n    momentum: float,\n    lr: float,\n    dampening: float,\n    nesterov: bool,\n    maximize: bool,\n):\n    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs SGD algorithm computation.\n\n    See :class:`~torch.optim.SGD` for details.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Respect when the user inputs False/True for foreach or fused.
